audience: ABA clinic owners and operations leaders evaluating a switch
title: CentralReach alternative — Infinite Suite OS™
seoTitle: CentralReach alternative | Recover Lost Hours | Infinite Sui
metaDescription: Evaluating CentralReach alternative? Keep your current EMR and add Infinite Suite OS™ beside it to recover cancellations and RBT callouts. Calculate your lost
h1: CentralReach alternative: recover the hours before you migrate
intro: If you're researching CentralReach alternative, you're probably trying to stop authorized hours from leaking out of the clinic. Before committing to a migration, the highest-leverage move is to measure the hours at risk and recover the workflow — beside the EMR you already run.

sections.heading: The operational problem
sections.body: Teams searching for CentralReach alternative are usually feeling the same operational pain: cancellations and staff callouts quietly turn authorized, billable hours into lost ones. For aba clinic owners and operations leaders evaluating a switch, the gap is rarely the EMR's data collection — it's the scramble after a session falls through, when there's no fast, fair way to route a makeup, cover a callout, keep the caregiver informed, and keep the documentation clean.

sections.heading: Why an EMR replacement may not solve this first
sections.body: Migrating to another full platform is expensive, slow, and disruptive — and it often doesn't fix the highest-friction gap, which is recovery after a cancellation. Compared with CentralReach, the faster win is operational: keep the EMR your clinic already runs on, and add Infinite Suite OS™ beside it as a recovery layer that measures and recovers the hours at risk before you take on a migration.

sections.heading: The recovery workflow
sections.body:
With a focus on subpool coverage and makeup routing, the recovery workflow runs the moment a session is at risk:

Cancellation or RBT callout → Scheduler AI™ → Auth Utilization / War Room → SubPool™ → FieldPocket™ → Care Pocket™ → Compliance Sentinel™ → API Integration Hub™.

One canceled session becomes one recovered, supported, documented, review-ready hour — beside your current EMR, not instead of it.

sections.heading: Calculate your lost-hours baseline
sections.body:
Before you switch EMRs, calculate your lost-hours baseline.

Shopping for ABA software? Before you migrate your clinic into another platform, estimate how many hours are being lost through cancellations, RBT callouts, poor recovery routing, caregiver communication gaps, and documentation cleanup.

Your EMR may track the session. Infinite Suite OS™ is built to help recover the session before it disappears.

faq.question: Is Infinite Suite OS™ a replacement for our current software?
faq.answer: No. Infinite Suite OS™ is an operational recovery layer that works beside your current EMR. You keep your existing system of record and add recovery, coverage, caregiver communication, and documentation-support workflows on top.

faq.question: Does it guarantee HIPAA compliance or billing correctness?
faq.answer: No tool can guarantee that. Infinite Suite OS™ supports compliance and documentation workflows and helps teams prepare cleaner, review-ready records, but every output requires human review, and billing and payer decisions stay with your qualified staff.

faq.question: How does it help with cancellations and RBT callouts?
faq.answer: When a session is canceled or a technician calls out, the recovery workflow routes the opening — a makeup, a substitute via SubPool™, or supported admin time — so the authorized hour has a fair chance to be recovered instead of silently lost.

faq.question: Do we have to migrate our data to get started?
faq.answer: No. The recovery layer is designed to sit beside your current platform, so you can measure the hours at risk and run recovery workflows without a full data migration.

faq.question: Is any patient information exposed in these tools?
faq.answer: No PHI is shown in the operational tools, and the workflows are built around de-identified operational data with human review before any action.

socialPosts.linkedinFounder:
I spent 10 years as an RBT before I built software — long enough to watch cancellations and callouts quietly become lost, unbillable hours.

If you're evaluating CentralReach alternative, here's the question I'd ask before migrating anything: how many authorized hours are you losing every month to cancellations and coverage gaps?

Keep your EMR. Add a recovery layer beside it. Measure the hours at risk first.

Calculate your lost-hours baseline → (link to /topics/centralreach-alternative-recovery-layer and the calculator)

complianceNotes: Operational estimates only. Infinite Suite OS™ supports compliance and documentation workflows but does not guarantee HIPAA compliance, payer approval, or billing correctness, and is not a medical, clinical, or billing decision-maker. No PHI is shown in these tools. All outputs require human review before action. Infinite Suite OS™ is an operational recovery layer that works beside your current EMR — it is not a full EMR replacement.
